title: Best of Atlanta Comedy Showcase
location: Laughing Skull Lounge, 878 Peachtree St NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Rotating lineup of Atlanta's best local comics — short sets mean non-stop laughs and easy conversation on the walk home. Intimate room, 21+, perfect mid-week date night.

title: Babatunde HipHopera: The HipHopera Tour
location: City Winery Atlanta, 650 North Ave NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Babatunde HipHopera blends hip-hop, opera and theater — a creative, conversation-starting show. Table seating and dinner service make it feel like a full date night in one ticket.

title: HANGEO Thursdays: Salsa & Bachata Night
location: HANGEO, Atlanta (Midtown) — check listing for exact address
description: Beginner-friendly social dance floor with rotating DJs. No partner needed — a low-pressure way to dance together (or learn) on a weeknight.

title: BLACK AF Rooftop Day Party — Atlanta's Official Juneteenth Celebration
location: AIR Rooftop Bar, 887 W Marietta St NW, Atlanta, GA
description: Rooftop Juneteenth party with DJs, dancing and skyline views. Dress up, grab cocktails, dance — an easy 'dinner plus party' date in one stop.

title: Culture Fest Day Party — Juneteenth Celebration
location: Cafe Circa Restaurant & Lounge, Atlanta, GA
description: Daytime Juneteenth celebration with music, food and dancing. Daytime pricing = cheaper cover, then you're home by sunset. Great first-date vibe.

title: Reggae on the Rooftop Festival — Juneteenth Weekend
location: AIR Rooftop (formerly Suite Lounge), Atlanta, GA
description: Six hours of reggae on a rooftop with food and drinks. Couples can split a drink, find shade, and vibe out — exactly the kind of low-effort, high-energy date that works.

title: Happy People Atlanta (Culture & Vibes Festival)
location: Underground Atlanta, 50 Upper Alabama St SW, Atlanta, GA
description: All-day Afrocentric culture festival at Underground Atlanta — music, vendors, food. Built for wandering together, easy to leave whenever you've had enough.

title: Claypool Gold (Primus / Les Claypool's Frog Brigade) at Chastain Park
location: Synovus Bank Amphitheater at Chastain Park, 4469 Stella Dr NW, Atlanta, GA
description: Picnic-style amphitheater show — bring a blanket and a bottle of wine. Outdoor summer concert under the trees is peak Atlanta date territory.

title: Atlanta BeltLine Fest (Free, 2-Day)
location: Pittsburgh Yards, Atlanta BeltLine
description: Free two-day festival with live music, DJs, food and World Cup matches on the big screen. Zero-cost, walk-around date — easy to do for an hour or the whole day.

title: Old Fourth Ward Arts Festival
location: Historic Fourth Ward Park, 680 North Ave NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Two-day juried art festival with 100+ artists, live music and food. Perfect 'stroll, snack, point at things you like' date with built-in things to talk about.

title: Salsa at Politan Row (Sundays)
location: Politan Row at Colony Square, 1197 Peachtree St NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Salsa & bachata lessons followed by social dancing in a food-hall courtyard. Grab tacos and a drink, then dance — one-stop Sunday afternoon date.

title: Vegan Soul Sunday — Real Men Cook Vegan (Father's Day Edition)
location: New Life Presbyterian Church, Atlanta, GA
description: Plant-based soul-food tasting event — Father's Day edition means big plates and good energy. Perfect for veggie-curious couples or anyone who likes to eat their way through a date.

title: Plant to the Future: Food, Freedom & Black Futures Conference
location: Pittsburgh Yards / The Food Studio, Atlanta, GA
description: Plant-based food conference with cooking demos, panels and tastings. A date for couples who like learning something together and leaving with new dinner ideas.

title: Lyle Lovett — Up Close: Songs & Stories
location: City Winery Atlanta, 650 North Ave NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Intimate, storytelling-style set with dinner service. Higher price tag, but the table-and-wine format makes it feel like a long, slow dinner-date with a concert built in.

title: illumine 2026 at Oakland Cemetery
location: Historic Oakland Cemetery, 248 Oakland Ave SE, Atlanta, GA
description: After-dark art-installation light show through Oakland Cemetery — romantic, walkable, $10 entry. One of the most uniquely 'Atlanta' date nights on the calendar.

title: Christina Kwan — 'Linger' Solo Exhibition Opening
time: 5:00 PM – 8:00 PM (opening; show runs through Aug 2)
location: Cat Eye Creative, 1173 Commerce Dr, Decatur, GA
description: Opening reception for a solo show in Decatur — easy to pair with dinner on the square. Free, low-pressure, art-date classic.

title: Georgia Artisan Fair
location: Historic Fourth Ward Skatepark, Atlanta, GA
description: Three-day maker market — jewelry, ceramics, prints, food. Built for browsing together and picking out something small as a keepsake from the date.

title: AFROXFEST — The African Experience Festival
location: Candler Park, Atlanta, GA
description: All-day African music, food and culture festival in Candler Park. Bring a blanket, eat your way around the vendors, dance when the music pulls you in.

title: Austin's Saturday Jazz Night at Serenbe
location: Austin's at Serenbe, 9110 Selborne Ln, Chattahoochee Hills, GA
description: Live jazz outside Atlanta in the romantic Serenbe hamlet. Worth the drive for couples who want a date-night escape — dinner on the town, then jazz under the stars.

title: Bourbon & Brisket Festival at The Battery Atlanta
location: The Battery Atlanta, 800 Battery Ave SE, Atlanta, GA
description: Bourbon, brisket and live music at The Battery — pairing well with a Braves day-game date or just strolling Truist Park's entertainment district.
